title.en: I don't know about that! 그건 좀 아닌 듯! 난 좀 다르게 생각해!
title.ko: 그건 좀 아닌 듯! 난 좀 다르게 생각해!

description.en: When you are not entirely convinced or have doubts about someone's opinion! Practice expressing your thoughts with 그건 좀 아닌 듯 and 난 좀 다르게 생각해.
description.ko: 상대방의 의견에 완전히 동의하기 어렵거나 의구심이 들 때! 그건 좀 아닌 듯과 난 좀 다르게 생각해를 사용해 조심스러우면서도 분명하게 내 의견을 전달해 봅니다.

tags: Reaction. Doubt. Skepticism. Different Opinion. Daily Life

content.speaker: John
content.text: What's up world! Welcome back. You are listening to... "Real Reaction!" I'm your host. John.

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 존. 저 방금 인터넷에서 봤는데. 매일 아침 차가운 얼음물로 샤워하면 건강에 진짜 좋대요! 우리 내일부터 같이 해볼까요?
content.translation: John. I just saw this on the internet. They say taking a cold ice water shower every morning is really good for your health! Shall we try it together starting tomorrow?

content.speaker: John
content.text: Oh... I don't know about that. 그건 좀 아닌 듯. 전 따뜻한 물로 샤워하는 게 건강에 더 좋다고 생각하거든요.

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 에이. 존은 너무 보수적이라니까요! 얼음물 샤워가 정신력 강화에도 최고라는데 한번 믿어보세요.
content.translation: Geez. You're so conservative. John! They say ice water showers are the best for strengthening mental power. so please trust me.

content.speaker: John
content.text: 미안하지만 난 좀 다르게 생각해. 정신력은 잠을 푹 자야 생기는 법이죠.
content.translation: I'm sorry. but I think a bit differently. Mental power comes from getting a good night's sleep.

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 역시 오늘도 우리 의견은 평행선이네요!
content.translation: As expected. our opinions are like parallel lines again today!

content.speaker: John
content.text: 그건 좀 아닌 듯. This phrase sounds a bit casual. right.

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 맞아요. 친구 사이에서 상대방의 의견이 나랑 다를 때. 혹은 그건 좀 이상하다고 느낄 때 가볍게 던질 수 있는 리액션이에요.
content.translation: Right. It's a reaction you can lightly toss out between friends when their opinion differs from yours or when you feel something is a bit strange.

content.speaker: John
content.text: 그건 좀 아닌 듯.

content.speaker: John
content.text: Then what about 난 좀 다르게 생각해.

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 이 표현은 조금 더 정중하고 차분하게 내 의견을 말할 때 써요. "네 생각도 존중하지만 내 생각은 이래" 라는 느낌이 담겨 있거든요.
content.translation: This expression is used when you state your opinion more politely and calmly. It carries the feeling of "I respect your thoughts. but this is what I think."

content.speaker: John
content.text: 난 좀 다르게 생각해.

content.speaker: John
content.text: Okay. let's try a situation. Imagine I'm saying that the best way to learn Korean is by only watching horror movies without subtitles.

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 존. 한국어를 배우는데 공포 영화만 본다고요? 자막도 없이요?
content.translation: John. watching only horror movies to learn Korean? Without subtitles?

content.speaker: John
content.text: Yeah! You'll learn the most important words like "살려줘!" very quickly.

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 존. 그건 잘 모르겠는데. 무서워서 공부가 전혀 안 될 것 같아요.
content.translation: John. I don't know about that. I think I won't be able to study at all because I'll be too scared.

content.speaker: John
content.text: But the emotions are so real! It's the best way. trust me.

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 미안하지만 난 좀 다르게 생각해.
content.translation: I'm sorry. but I think a bit differently.

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 차라리 재미있는 드라마를 보면서 천천히 배우는 게 더 효과적일 것 같아요.
content.translation: I think it would be more effective to learn slowly while watching fun dramas.

content.speaker: John
content.text: Okay. I see your point. Maybe horror movies are a bit too much.

content.speaker: John
content.text: It's good to be honest when you're not sure about someone's idea.

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 맞아요. 무조건 맞다고 하기보다 내 생각을 솔직하게 표현하는 게 더 좋은 대화를 만드는 비결이에요.
content.translation: Right. Being honest about your thoughts rather than just agreeing is the secret to making better conversations.

content.speaker: John
content.text: Let's review our "not sure" reactions one last time.

content.speaker: John
content.text: 그건 좀 아닌 듯! That seems a bit off!

content.speaker: John
content.text: 난 좀 다르게 생각해! I think a bit differently!

content.speaker: John
content.text: 그건 잘 모르겠는데! I'm not so sure about that!

content.speaker: John
content.text: 내 생각은 조금 달라! My thoughts are a bit different!

content.speaker: John
content.text: 글쎄. 난 반대야! Well. I'm against it!

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 여러분도 오늘 친구의 의견에 의문이 생긴다면 당황하지 말고 오늘 배운 표현들을 써보세요!
content.translation: Everyone. if you have doubts about a friend's opinion today. don't be flustered and try using the expressions we learned today!

content.speaker: John
content.text: That's all for today. See you in the next episode.

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 다음에 또 만나요. 안녕~!
content.translation: See you again next time. Bye~!

vocabulary.example: 그 계획에 대해서는 약간의 의구심이 들어요. (I have some doubts about that plan.)
우리 부모님은 생각보다 보수적이세요. (My parents are more conservative than I thought.)
두 사람의 의견은 좁혀지지 않고 평행선을 달리고 있어요. (The two people's opinions are not narrowing and are running like parallel lines.)
존은 항상 정중한 태도로 말해요. (John always speaks with a polite attitude.)
체력 강화를 위해 매일 운동을 해요. (I exercise every day to strengthen my physical strength.)
이 방법이 훨씬 더 효과적인 것 같아요. (I think this method is much more effective.)
